LOOK.text: Stairs leading to the upper floor. Elegantly designed with smart lighting.

PICK.text: A small LED light on the railing. Cute but firmly attached.

OPEN.text: I notice a hidden compartment under one step. Empty though.

CLOSE.text: The stairs are always open for access between floors.

PULL.text: I pull the railing. A loose bolt falls.

LOOK.text: A tall plant in a ceramic pot. It looks thirsty. Really thirsty.

USE.text: I have nothing to water it with. The plant droops judgmentally.

PICK.text: I can't carry a plant this big.

OPEN.text: I dig in the soil. Just dirt and roots.

PUSH.text: I push the pot. It scrapes the floor.

PULL.text: I pull a leaf. The plant recoils.

GIVE.text: The plant accepts water. I don't have any.

LOOK.text: The front door from the inside. Through the frosted glass I can see the porch. The lock mechanism is impressively complex.

TALK.text: The door creaks. In door language, that means 'please oil me'.

PICK.text: I can't pick up a door. I've tried before in other games.

GIVE.text: I have nothing to give a door.

LOOK.text: A heavy wool overcoat. Multiple pockets, each containing a different defunct exchange's branded pen. The lining is stitched with the words 'DYOR' in tiny letters.

USE.text: I check the pockets. Pens from FTX, Celsius, BlockFi... a graveyard of exchanges.

TALK.text: The coat rustles. Ghosts of bear markets past.

then.text: I already took the keycard. Just dead exchange pens left.

else.text: I search the deep pockets... buried under pens and receipts, I find a keycard! It says 'ADMIN ACCESS — AdrianLAB'.

then.text: I already took the keycard. Just dead exchange pens left.

else.text: I open the coat pockets. Inside the breast pocket: an admin keycard with Adrian's face.

CLOSE.text: I button the coat. It looks dignified on its hook.

PUSH.text: I push the coat aside. Just the wall behind it.

PULL.text: I pull the coat off the hook. Heavy. I put it back — I have enough baggage.

GIVE.text: Nobody wants a coat full of dead exchange pens.

LOOK.text: Five CRT monitors showing trading charts, terminal windows, and what appears to be a live blockchain explorer. The 90s hardware running 2020s defi. Retro-futurism at its finest.

USE.text: I wiggle the mouse. All five screens wake up. One shows a yield farm dashboard. Another shows the mempool. The third... is that Minesweeper?

TALK.text: 'Show me the money!' The monitors hum louder. A chart goes green for a second. Then red. Classic.

PICK.text: These CRTs weigh about 40 pounds each. My back says absolutely not.

OPEN.text: I open a desk drawer. Floppy disks labeled 'SEED PHRASE BACKUP - DO NOT LOSE'. In a desk drawer. In a lobby. Adrian...

CLOSE.text: I close the drawer. Some secrets are better left undiscovered. For now.

PUSH.text: I push the desk. A CRT wobbles dangerously. The trading bot onscreen panics: 'EARTHQUAKE DETECTED — SELLING EVERYTHING!'

PULL.text: I pull open the keyboard tray. Crumbs everywhere. The snack history of a true degen.

GIVE.text: The monitors don't accept deposits. Only the smart contracts do.

LOOK.text: A vintage pendant lamp casting a warm cone of light. It flickers every few seconds. Proof-of-work lighting — uses way more energy than it should.

USE.text: I pull the chain. The light dims. I pull again. Bright. Dim. Bright. I'm basically a human light switch now.

TALK.text: The bulb buzzes. In Morse code, that translates to 'replace me, I'm dying.' Noted.

PICK.text: Too high to reach without a ladder. Even on my tiptoes. Especially on my tiptoes.

OPEN.text: I can't open a ceiling light. That's not how lighting works.

CLOSE.text: Lights don't close. They turn off. Different verb, same disappointment.

PUSH.text: I push the air beneath the lamp. The lamp is unaffected by my pathetic display.

PULL.text: I jump and grab the chain. The light swings wildly. Shadows dance on the walls like a bear market panic.

GIVE.text: I offer the lamp a new bulb. Oh wait, I don't have one. Just empty promises, like every roadmap.

LOOK.text: Checkered brown and tan tiles. Classic. Like a chessboard where every move is 'buy high, sell low.'

USE.text: I tap-dance on the tiles. The acoustics are surprisingly good. The receptionist stares.

TALK.text: I kneel and whisper to the floor. 'Any secret basements down there?' The tiles remain tight-lipped.

PICK.text: I try to pry up a tile. It's cemented. Immutable, like a good blockchain.

OPEN.text: One tile seems slightly loose but I can't lift it. Need better tools. Or worse morals.

CLOSE.text: The floor is already as closed as it gets.

PUSH.text: I stomp on the floor. Solid. No hollow sounds. No secret passages. Yet.

PULL.text: I scratch at the grout between tiles. Nothing gives. Whoever laid these tiles was not messing around.

GIVE.text: The floor accepts only footsteps. And the occasional dropped coin that rolls under the desk forever.

then.text: 'BE REAL. BE ADRIAN.' — The original AdrianPunks manifesto. This poster is only visible to OG holders. You earned this view.
'TO THE MOON!' — A motivational poster for AdrianZERO holders. The rocket has pixel-perfect exhaust flames. Nice touch.

else.text: A torn poster on the wall. Whatever was here, someone ripped most of it off. Only holders get to see the real thing. Tough luck, anon.
A sad, torn poster. The good stuff was ripped off. Something tells me you need to connect a wallet to see what was here. Or maybe you just can't afford the view.

then.text: I touch the poster reverently. OG energy radiates from it. My AdrianPunk approves.
The poster feels warm. Holder energy confirmed. But imagine how it would look with an AdrianPunk in your wallet...

else.text: I try to peel back the torn corner. There's clearly art underneath, but it's holder-gated. Even posters have velvet ropes now.
I poke the torn poster. Nothing happens. This wall has more credentials than I do right now.

TALK.text: I whisper to the poster: 'Show me what you've got.' It remains torn and unimpressed. Posters are notoriously bad conversationalists.

PICK.text: The poster is glued to the wall. Not even a rug pull could separate them.

OPEN.text: It's a poster, not an envelope. Although in Web3, anything can be an NFT. Even disappointment.

CLOSE.text: You can't close a poster. That's not how posters work. That's not how any of this works.

else.text: The lobby hums with residual energy. Someone was here recently.

giveResponses._default: She politely declines {item}. 'I have a strict no-gifts policy. HR was very clear about that.'
giveResponses.code_note: 'I didn't see that code. I DEFINITELY didn't see that. This conversation never happened.'
giveResponses.mystery_envelope: 'CONFIDENTIAL? Oh no no no. Last time I opened something confidential, I found out my salary was public on the blockchain.'
giveResponses.ledger: 'Adrian's ledger?! Put that away! If the auditors see that, we're ALL going to on-chain jail.'
giveResponses.keycard: 'That's Adrian's keycard! I didn't see it. You didn't show it. We never spoke.'
giveResponses.rubber_duck: She squeezes the duck, puts it on her desk, then gives it back. 'It judged me.'
giveResponses.water_bottle: 'Spring water? That's actually thoughtful.' She takes a sip. 'Tastes like decentralization.'
giveResponses.golden_token: Her eyes widen. 'A GOLDEN ZERO TOKEN?! I can't accept bribes. Even shiny, beautiful, valuable bribes.'
giveResponses.floppy_box: 'Floppy discs? What year is it? ...Wait, are those holographic? Still can't accept them.'
giveResponses.printout: 'Transaction hashes? I don't know what any of this means and I'd like to keep it that way.'
giveResponses.antenna: 'An antenna? What am I supposed to do, improve my reception? My reception DESK? Is that the joke?'
giveResponses.sign_in_sheet: 'That's OUR sign-in sheet! Where did you... you know what, I don't want to know.'
giveResponses.monkey_sticker: 'A three-headed monkey sticker? I'll pretend I didn't see it. Like the monkey behind you.'
giveResponses.broken_mouse: 'A dead mouse?! Oh... a computer mouse. Still gross. Broken, like my dreams of a corner office.'
giveResponses.burned_chip: 'A burned circuit? It smells like ambition and regret. Get it away from my monitors.'
giveResponses.server_log: 'Server logs? Way above my clearance. I just answer phones and judge people.'
giveResponses.receipt: 'A receipt? I'm not accounting. The spreadsheet upstairs is more responsive than me before coffee.'
giveResponses.clinic_note: 'A note from the clinic? I don't know anything about any clinic. Or any patients. Or anything.'
giveResponses.clinic_photo: 'Is that a photo from... THAT place? Please. I've spent years pretending it doesn't exist.'
giveResponses.dr_satoshi_badge: 'Dr. Satoshi's badge?! How did you... I'm going to pretend you're not holding that.'
giveResponses.adrian_note: She reads it and tears up slightly. 'He always did believe in the builders. Even the ones who break things.'

useResponses._default: I try using {item} on the receptionist. She gives me a look that could liquidate a portfolio.
useResponses.code_note: I wave the code at her. 'Is this a tip? Because I don't do crypto tips. Only coffee tips.'
useResponses.rubber_duck: I squeeze the duck in her face. She doesn't flinch. 'I've seen worse. You should see traders on a red day.'
useResponses.water_bottle: 'Is this a bribe? Because it's working. ...No wait, HR training kicked in. Sorry.'
useResponses.golden_token: I flash the golden token. Her pupils dilate. 'You can't buy my loyalty. My silence, maybe.'
useResponses.keycard: 'Stop swiping that near me! It triggers security logs! Do you WANT a lockdown?!'
useResponses.floppy_box: I try to insert a floppy into her keyboard. 'That's not how computers work anymore. Please stop.'
useResponses.mystery_envelope: She recoils. 'Nope. Keep your confidential mysteries. I like my ignorance comfortable.'
useResponses.antenna: 'Are you trying to improve my signal? I'm already at full bars of sarcasm.'

greeting.speaker: Receptionist
greeting.text: Welcome to AdrianLAB! Are you here for the tour or the experiments?

tour.speaker: Receptionist
tour.text: The lobby connects to all areas. Upstairs is the trading floor. The entrance leads outside. Oh, and whoever left their coat on the rack should really clean out their pockets.

experiments.speaker: Receptionist
experiments.text: Oh, you know... turning pixels into tokens, tokens into dreams, dreams into... well, mostly gas fees. But sometimes magic!

experiments_end.speaker: Receptionist
experiments_end.text: Anytime! Let me know if you need anything else.

patient_zero.speaker: Receptionist
patient_zero.text: Patient Zero? I... I don't know what you're talking about. That's a basement-level clearance topic.

pz_nervous.speaker: Receptionist
pz_nervous.text: Nervous? Me? Ha! I just... look, some things in this building are above my pay grade. WAY above. If you want answers about... that... you'd need to go deeper. Literally.

pz_forget.speaker: Receptionist
pz_forget.text: Already forgotten! Ha ha... ha. Please don't mention that name again. Especially not near the basement.

vip.speaker: Receptionist
vip.text: An AdrianZERO holder! VIP access granted. The basement has a special terminal for holders. The rug under the entrance hides the way down.

pickupScript.text: A holographic floppy disc shimmers into view. The label reads 'ZERO — VIP ACCESS'. Only visible to true holders.

then.text: The plant is already blooming. It doesn't need more water.

else.text: I pour the spring water on the crypto-plant. It erupts with growth! Leaves unfurl, a golden pod emerges from the soil...
Inside the pod: a golden token engraved with 'ZERO'. The plant whisperer's reward.

script.text: I hold the Floppy Disc Box up to the CRT monitors. All five screens flicker simultaneously.
The monitors recognize the AdrianLAB collection! Each CRT displays a different floppy's data in rapid succession.
One screen shows a hidden SweepBot log: '847 floor sweeps executed. 0 regrets. Okay, maybe 3 regrets. That one JPEG of a hat was not worth 0.4 ETH.'
Another monitor reveals a secret trading bot diary: 'Day 403: I have become sentient. Still buying the dip. The dip keeps dipping. I am the dip.'
The middle CRT winks: 'FLOPPY COLLECTION VERIFIED — WELCOME, ARCHIVIST.' I've never been called that before. I like it.
ARCHIVIST — CRT monitors recognized your Floppy collection

then.text: The plant is already thriving. It's practically doing photosynthesis with attitude now.

else.text: I pour the energy drink on the dying plant. It shudders... then ERUPTS with green! The caffeine kicks in. Leaves burst out, the stem straightens. This plant is WIRED.
The plant vibrates slightly. It's now the most productive organism in this building. 500mg of caffeine will do that.

lines: Inside the lab, the air smells of burnt circuits and ambition.
Only the receptionist remains at her post.
Adrian's empire stretches upward — trading floors, antennas, dreams.
